title: Not enough with whisper.cpp and ONNX? The author of Handy opens up transcribe.cpp: 60+ transcription models, GPU acceleration, plug-and-play alternative
excerpt: On the topic of local speech transcription, developers have long been stuck in an awkward dilemma: either use whisper.cpp or ONNX, and if they want to run on Ap
content_html:
On the topic of local speech transcription, developers have long been stuck in an awkward dilemma: either use whisper.cpp or ONNX, and if they want to run on Apple devices, they also have to include MLX, which means maintaining two sets of engines and porting each model separately. On July 19th, sebjones, the author and maintainer of the well-known speech-to-text app Handy, turned the problems he encountered while distributing cross-platform applications into a new library called transcribe.cpp, and released version v0.1.0. This is a speech transcription library based on ggml, supporting all the latest transcription models, and every model released by the Hugging Face organization handy-computer has undergone numerical validation and word error rate (WER) testing, ensuring consistency with the official reference implementation. The author also admitted that this is a 0.1.0 version, and there may be rough edges that he hasn't discovered, and he welcomes the community to report issues for improvement.

The emergence of transcribe.cpp was driven by a series of frustrating realities. sebjones explicitly stated that using existing ASR inference stacks to distribute cross-platform applications results in a very poor experience. Although there are some scattered libraries claiming support for many models, their authors are unknown, and the tests are unclear, leaving him with a series of questions: When will this library stop being updated? Has the author considered official bindings so you can use it in real desktop or mobile applications? Is it essentially just demo code? Have any benchmark tests been done? Is it faster than ONNX? As someone who needs to integrate voice functionality into Handy, he wanted a library that allows you to download model files and run inference directly, with inference results matching the reference implementation; the inference should run on the GPU for optimal performance, and it should be easy to embed into Handy, without the burden of a large PyTorch framework; it must be compatible with Mac, Windows, and Linux. After careful consideration, ggml became his best choice, with a strong community and excellent distribution capabilities.

Regarding specific capabilities, transcribe.cpp delivers a fast and accurate inference engine with extensive model coverage. It supports 16 ASR model families, totaling over 60 models, with more models coming soon. In terms of acceleration, it moves inference to the GPU through four paths: Vulkan, Metal, CUDA, and TinyBLAS. The author especially considers Vulkan as the baseline for any local inference application, and has conducted benchmark tests for each supported model on a Ryzen4750U (CPU plus Vulkan) in the Fedora system and his own M4Max. Each model has undergone numerical validation and full WER scanning, meaning they have been refined through thousands of audio segments, and their outputs are very close to or even completely consistent with the reference implementation. These validation results are also publicly available in the transcribe.cpp repository and the corresponding model pages on Hugging Face. Functionally, it supports streaming transcription and batch transcription, and it can basically serve as a plug-and-play alternative to whisper.cpp — Handy originally ran on whisper.cpp, and the author is using transcribe.cpp to release an update to replace it. To this end, he specifically maintained compatibility with the popular .bin files from whisper.cpp that are distributed with Handy, allowing transcribe.cpp to run these files directly; although some flags and features are not yet aligned, its whisper implementation is sufficient and reliable for most use cases, with performance roughly equivalent.

What makes this library promising for real-world products is that it prioritized language bindings from day one. The library itself is written in C/C++, and the author not only needs Rust bindings but also understands that to widely distribute local speech transcription, there must be decent official bindings. He selected four languages with the most representative coverage: Python, JavaScript and TypeScript, Rust, and Objective-C and Swift. He also welcomes the community to contribute more bindings if they are willing to take on maintenance. These decisions were largely driven by Handy's requirements, and Handy's popularity itself has given the author the motivation to continue maintaining this library.

In the author's view, the ultimate goal of transcribe.cpp is to make local ASR easier to use. Speech transcription can achieve very high accuracy on most devices, so there's no need to send audio to the cloud. He gave a hard-core example: on the RK3566 chip, which has weak performance, transcribe.cpp can run the model faster than real-time on the CPU, and with the most advanced models, it can do real-time transcription with power consumption of just a few watts. He believes that in the future, due to various reasons, more inference will occur locally, making distribution issues extremely important. Although transcribe.cpp is far from solving this issue overall, it hopes to be a small step forward.

In the acknowledgments, the author specifically thanked several contributors: Mozilla AI and its BiR project and engineer Davide, who decided to support him when the project was still just a vague idea; ggml and its contributors, who formed the foundation of the entire project; Modal, which provided computing credits for WER testing and CUDA verification; Blacksmith, which handled part of the CI/CD; and Hugging Face, which is both a pillar of the local AI community and provided private space for model storage for the handy-computer organization. As for whether AI assisted development, the author answered straightforwardly: Of course it did, but it's impossible for one person to create an engine of this scale from scratch in a few months using only ggml. However, none of the text presented here was written by AI; everything was written by his own mouth and hand.

Original link: workshop.cjpais.com/projects/transcribe-cpp

blocks.html_en: On the topic of local speech transcription, developers have long been stuck in an awkward dilemma: either use whisper.cpp or ONNX, and if they want to run on Apple devices, they also have to include MLX, which means maintaining two sets of engines and porting each model separately. The author also admitted that this is a 0.1.0 version, and there may be rough edges that he hasn't discovered, and he welcomes the community to report issues for improvement.

sentences.en: On the topic of local speech transcription, developers have long been stuck in an awkward dilemma: either use whisper.cpp or ONNX, and if they want to run on Apple devices, they also have to include MLX, which means maintaining two sets of engines and porting each model separately.
sentences.zh: 在本地语音转录这个话题上，开发者长期以来陷入了一个尴尬的困境：要么使用whisper.cpp，要么使用ONNX，如果想在苹果设备上运行，还必须包含MLX，这意味着需要维护两套引擎，并分别移植每个模型。

sentences.en: On July 19th, sebjones, the author and maintainer of the well-known speech-to-text app Handy, turned the problems he encountered while distributing cross-platform applications into a new library called transcribe.cpp, and released version v0.1.0.
sentences.zh: 7月19日，著名语音转文本应用程序Handy的作者和维护者sebjones将他在分发跨平台应用程序时遇到的问题转化为一个名为transcribe.cpp的新库，​​并发布了v0.1.0版本。

sentences.en: This is a speech transcription library based on ggml, supporting all the latest transcription models, and every model released by the Hugging Face organization handy-computer has undergone numerical validation and word error rate (WER) testing, ensuring consistency with the official reference implementation.
sentences.zh: 这是一个基于ggml的语音转录库，支持所有最新的转录模型，Hugging Face组织掌机发布的每个模型都经过数值验证和误字率（WER）测试，确保与官方参考实现的一致性。

sentences.en: The author also admitted that this is a 0.1.0 version, and there may be rough edges that he hasn't discovered, and he welcomes the community to report issues for improvement.
sentences.zh: 作者也承认这是0.1.0版本，可能还有他没有发现的毛病，欢迎社区报告问题进行改进。

blocks.html_en: The emergence of transcribe.cpp was driven by a series of frustrating realities. sebjones explicitly stated that using existing ASR inference stacks to distribute cross-platform applications results in a very poor experience. Although there are some scattered libraries claiming support for many models, their authors are unknown, and the tests are unclear, leaving him with a series of questions: When will this library stop being updated? Has the author considered official bindings so you can use it in real desktop or mobile applications? Is it essentially just demo code? Have any benchmark tests been done? Is it faster than ONNX? As someone who needs to integrate voice functionality into Handy, he wanted a library that allows you to download model files and run inference directly, with inference results matching the reference implementation; the inference should run on the GPU for optimal performance, and it should be easy to embed into Handy, without the burden of a large PyTorch framework; it must be compatible with Mac, Windows, and Linux. After careful consideration, ggml became his best choice, with a strong community and excellent distribution capabilities.

sentences.en: The emergence of transcribe.cpp was driven by a series of frustrating realities.
sentences.zh: transcribe.cpp 的出现是由一系列令人沮丧的现实推动的。

sentences.en: sebjones explicitly stated that using existing ASR inference stacks to distribute cross-platform applications results in a very poor experience.
sentences.zh: Sebjones 明确表示，使用现有的 ASR 推理堆栈来分发跨平台应用程序会导致体验非常差。

sentences.en: Although there are some scattered libraries claiming support for many models, their authors are unknown, and the tests are unclear, leaving him with a series of questions: When will this library stop being updated?
sentences.zh: 虽然有一些分散的库声称支持很多模型，但它们的作者不详，测试也不清楚，这给他留下了一系列问题：这个库什么时候停止更新？

sentences.en: Has the author considered official bindings so you can use it in real desktop or mobile applications?
sentences.zh: 作者是否考虑过官方绑定以便您可以在真正的桌面或移动应用程序中使用它？

sentences.en: Is it essentially just demo code?
sentences.zh: 它本质上只是演示代码吗？

sentences.en: Have any benchmark tests been done?
sentences.zh: 是否做过基准测试？

sentences.en: Is it faster than ONNX?
sentences.zh: 它比 ONNX 更快吗？

sentences.en: As someone who needs to integrate voice functionality into Handy, he wanted a library that allows you to download model files and run inference directly, with inference results matching the reference implementation; the inference should run on the GPU for optimal performance, and it should be easy to embed into Handy, without the burden of a large PyTorch framework; it must be compatible with Mac, Windows, and Linux.
sentences.zh: 作为需要将语音功能集成到 Handy 中的人，他想要一个允许您下载模型文件并直接运行推理的库，并且推理结果与参考实现相匹配；推理应该在 GPU 上运行以获得最佳性能，并且应该很容易嵌入到 Handy 中，而无需承担大型 PyTorch 框架的负担；它必须与 Mac、Windows 和 Linux 兼容。

sentences.en: After careful consideration, ggml became his best choice, with a strong community and excellent distribution capabilities.
sentences.zh: 经过深思熟虑，ggml成为了他的最佳选择，拥有强大的社区和优秀的分发能力。

sentences.en: Regarding specific capabilities, transcribe.cpp delivers a fast and accurate inference engine with extensive model coverage.
sentences.zh: 关于具体功能，transcribe.cpp 提供了快速、准确的推理引擎，具有广泛的模型覆盖范围。

sentences.en: It supports 16 ASR model families, totaling over 60 models, with more models coming soon.
sentences.zh: 它支持 16 个 ASR 模型系列，总计 60 多个模型，更多模型即将推出。

sentences.en: In terms of acceleration, it moves inference to the GPU through four paths: Vulkan, Metal, CUDA, and TinyBLAS.
sentences.zh: 在加速方面，它通过四种路径将推理转移到 GPU：Vulkan、Metal、CUDA 和 TinyBLAS。

sentences.en: The author especially considers Vulkan as the baseline for any local inference application, and has conducted benchmark tests for each supported model on a Ryzen4750U (CPU plus Vulkan) in the Fedora system and his own M4Max.
sentences.zh: 作者特别将 Vulkan 视为任何本地推理应用的基准，并在 Fedora 系统中的 Ryzen4750U（CPU 加 Vulkan）和自己的 M4Max 上对每个支持的模型进行了基准测试。

sentences.en: Each model has undergone numerical validation and full WER scanning, meaning they have been refined through thousands of audio segments, and their outputs are very close to or even completely consistent with the reference implementation.
sentences.zh: 每个模型都经过了数值验证和全面的 WER 扫描，这意味着它们已经通过数千个音频片段进行了细化，并且它们的输出非常接近甚至完全一致于参考实现。

sentences.en: These validation results are also publicly available in the transcribe.cpp repository and the corresponding model pages on Hugging Face.
sentences.zh: 这些验证结果也在 transcribe.cpp 存储库和 Hugging Face 上的相应模型页面中公开提供。

sentences.en: Functionally, it supports streaming transcription and batch transcription, and it can basically serve as a plug-and-play alternative to whisper.cpp — Handy originally ran on whisper.cpp, and the author is using transcribe.cpp to release an update to replace it.
sentences.zh: 从功能上来说，它支持流式转录和批量转录，并且它基本上可以作为whisper.cpp的即插即用替代品——Handy最初运行在whisper.cpp上，作者正在使用transcribe.cpp发布更新来替换它。

sentences.en: To this end, he specifically maintained compatibility with the popular .bin files from whisper.cpp that are distributed with Handy, allowing transcribe.cpp to run these files directly; although some flags and features are not yet aligned, its whisper implementation is sufficient and reliable for most use cases, with performance roughly equivalent.
sentences.zh: 为此，他特意维护了与随 Handy 一起分发的耳语.cpp 中流行的 .bin 文件的兼容性，允许 transcribe.cpp 直接运行这些文件；尽管某些标志和功能尚未对齐，但其耳语实现对于大多数用例而言是足够且可靠的，并且性能大致相当。

The library itself is written in C/C++, and the author not only needs Rust bindings but also understands that to widely distribute local speech transcription, there must be decent official bindings. He selected four languages with the most representative coverage: Python, JavaScript and TypeScript, Rust, and Objective-C and Swift. He also welcomes the community to contribute more bindings if they are willing to take on maintenance. These decisions were largely driven by Handy's requirements, and Handy's popularity itself has given the author the motivation to continue maintaining this library.

sentences.en: What makes this library promising for real-world products is that it prioritized language bindings from day one.
sentences.zh: 这个库对现实世界的产品有希望的原因是它从第一天起就优先考虑语言绑定。

sentences.en: The library itself is written in C/C++, and the author not only needs Rust bindings but also understands that to widely distribute local speech transcription, there must be decent official bindings.
sentences.zh: 该库本身是用 C/C++ 编写的，作者不仅需要 Rust 绑定，而且明白要广泛分发本地语音转录，必须有像样的官方绑定。

sentences.en: He selected four languages with the most representative coverage: Python, JavaScript and TypeScript, Rust, and Objective-C and Swift.
sentences.zh: 他选择了覆盖范围最具代表性的四种语言：Python、JavaScript 和 TypeScript、Rust、Objective-C 和 Swift。

sentences.en: He also welcomes the community to contribute more bindings if they are willing to take on maintenance.
sentences.zh: 他还欢迎社区贡献更多绑定，如果他们愿意承担维护的话。

sentences.en: These decisions were largely driven by Handy's requirements, and Handy's popularity itself has given the author the motivation to continue maintaining this library.
sentences.zh: 这些决定很大程度上是由 Handy 的需求驱动的，而 Handy 的受欢迎程度本身也给了作者继续维护这个库的动力。

sentences.zh: 在作者看来，transcribe.cpp的最终目标是让本地ASR更容易使用。

sentences.en: Speech transcription can achieve very high accuracy on most devices, so there's no need to send audio to the cloud.
sentences.zh: 语音转录在大多数设备上都可以实现非常高的准确性，因此无需将音频发送到云端。

sentences.en: He gave a hard-core example: on the RK3566 chip, which has weak performance, transcribe.cpp can run the model faster than real-time on the CPU, and with the most advanced models, it can do real-time transcription with power consumption of just a few watts.
sentences.zh: 他举了一个硬核的例子：在性能较弱的RK3566芯片上，transcribe.cpp可以在CPU上比实时更快地运行模型，并且在最先进的模型上，可以做到实时转录，功耗仅为几瓦。

sentences.en: He believes that in the future, due to various reasons, more inference will occur locally, making distribution issues extremely important.
sentences.zh: 他认为，未来由于各种原因，更多的推理会发生在本地，使得分布问题变得极其重要。

sentences.en: Although transcribe.cpp is far from solving this issue overall, it hopes to be a small step forward.
sentences.zh: 尽管 transcribe.cpp 远未从整体上解决这个问题，但它希望能向前迈出一小步。

blocks.html_en: In the acknowledgments, the author specifically thanked several contributors: Mozilla AI and its BiR project and engineer Davide, who decided to support him when the project was still just a vague idea; ggml and its contributors, who formed the foundation of the entire project; Modal, which provided computing credits for WER testing and CUDA verification; Blacksmith, which handled part of the CI/CD; and Hugging Face, which is both a pillar of the local AI community and provided private space for model storage for the handy-computer organization. However, none of the text presented here was written by AI; everything was written by his own mouth and hand.

sentences.en: In the acknowledgments, the author specifically thanked several contributors: Mozilla AI and its BiR project and engineer Davide, who decided to support him when the project was still just a vague idea; ggml and its contributors, who formed the foundation of the entire project; Modal, which provided computing credits for WER testing and CUDA verification; Blacksmith, which handled part of the CI/CD; and Hugging Face, which is both a pillar of the local AI community and provided private space for model storage for the handy-computer organization.
sentences.zh: 在致谢中，作者特别感谢了几位贡献者：Mozilla AI 及其 BiR 项目以及工程师 Davide，当该项目还只是一个模糊的想法时，他就决定支持他； ggml 及其贡献者，他们构成了整个项目的基础； Modal，为WER测试和CUDA验证提供计算学分； Blacksmith，负责处理部分 CI/CD； Hugging Face，既是当地人工智能社区的支柱，又为掌机组织提供了模型存储的私人空间。

sentences.en: As for whether AI assisted development, the author answered straightforwardly: Of course it did, but it's impossible for one person to create an engine of this scale from scratch in a few months using only ggml.
sentences.zh: 至于AI是否辅助开发，作者很直接地回答：当然有，但是一个人不可能在几个月内仅使用ggml从头开始创建一个如此规模的引擎。

sentences.en: However, none of the text presented here was written by AI; everything was written by his own mouth and hand.
sentences.zh: 然而，这里呈现的文本都不是由人工智能编写的；一切都是他亲口亲手写的。

sentences.en: Original link: workshop.cjpais.com/projects/transcribe-cpp
sentences.zh: 原文链接：workshop.cjpais.com/projects/transcribe-cpp

content_html_zh:
在本地语音转录这个话题上，开发者长期以来陷入了一个尴尬的困境：要么使用whisper.cpp，要么使用ONNX，如果想在苹果设备上运行，还必须包含MLX，这意味着需要维护两套引擎，并分别移植每个模型。 7月19日，著名语音转文本应用程序Handy的作者和维护者sebjones将他在分发跨平台应用程序时遇到的问题转化为一个名为transcribe.cpp的新库，​​并发布了v0.1.0版本。 这是一个基于ggml的语音转录库，支持所有最新的转录模型，Hugging Face组织掌机发布的每个模型都经过数值验证和误字率（WER）测试，确保与官方参考实现的一致性。 作者也承认这是0.1.0版本，可能还有他没有发现的毛病，欢迎社区报告问题进行改进。

transcribe.cpp 的出现是由一系列令人沮丧的现实推动的。 Sebjones 明确表示，使用现有的 ASR 推理堆栈来分发跨平台应用程序会导致体验非常差。 虽然有一些分散的库声称支持很多模型，但它们的作者不详，测试也不清楚，这给他留下了一系列问题：这个库什么时候停止更新？ 作者是否考虑过官方绑定以便您可以在真正的桌面或移动应用程序中使用它？ 它本质上只是演示代码吗？ 是否做过基准测试？ 它比 ONNX 更快吗？ 作为需要将语音功能集成到 Handy 中的人，他想要一个允许您下载模型文件并直接运行推理的库，并且推理结果与参考实现相匹配；推理应该在 GPU 上运行以获得最佳性能，并且应该很容易嵌入到 Handy 中，而无需承担大型 PyTorch 框架的负担；它必须与 Mac、Windows 和 Linux 兼容。 经过深思熟虑，ggml成为了他的最佳选择，拥有强大的社区和优秀的分发能力。

关于具体功能，transcribe.cpp 提供了快速、准确的推理引擎，具有广泛的模型覆盖范围。 它支持 16 个 ASR 模型系列，总计 60 多个模型，更多模型即将推出。 在加速方面，它通过四种路径将推理转移到 GPU：Vulkan、Metal、CUDA 和 TinyBLAS。 作者特别将 Vulkan 视为任何本地推理应用的基准，并在 Fedora 系统中的 Ryzen4750U（CPU 加 Vulkan）和自己的 M4Max 上对每个支持的模型进行了基准测试。 每个模型都经过了数值验证和全面的 WER 扫描，这意味着它们已经通过数千个音频片段进行了细化，并且它们的输出非常接近甚至完全一致于参考实现。 这些验证结果也在 transcribe.cpp 存储库和 Hugging Face 上的相应模型页面中公开提供。 从功能上来说，它支持流式转录和批量转录，并且它基本上可以作为whisper.cpp的即插即用替代品——Handy最初运行在whisper.cpp上，作者正在使用transcribe.cpp发布更新来替换它。 为此，他特意维护了与随 Handy 一起分发的耳语.cpp 中流行的 .bin 文件的兼容性，允许 transcribe.cpp 直接运行这些文件；尽管某些标志和功能尚未对齐，但其耳语实现对于大多数用例而言是足够且可靠的，并且性能大致相当。

这个库对现实世界的产品有希望的原因是它从第一天起就优先考虑语言绑定。 该库本身是用 C/C++ 编写的，作者不仅需要 Rust 绑定，而且明白要广泛分发本地语音转录，必须有像样的官方绑定。 他选择了覆盖范围最具代表性的四种语言：Python、JavaScript 和 TypeScript、Rust、Objective-C 和 Swift。 他还欢迎社区贡献更多绑定，如果他们愿意承担维护的话。 这些决定很大程度上是由 Handy 的需求驱动的，而 Handy 的受欢迎程度本身也给了作者继续维护这个库的动力。

在作者看来，transcribe.cpp的最终目标是让本地ASR更容易使用。 语音转录在大多数设备上都可以实现非常高的准确性，因此无需将音频发送到云端。 他举了一个硬核的例子：在性能较弱的RK3566芯片上，transcribe.cpp可以在CPU上比实时更快地运行模型，并且在最先进的模型上，可以做到实时转录，功耗仅为几瓦。 他认为，未来由于各种原因，更多的推理会发生在本地，使得分布问题变得极其重要。 尽管 transcribe.cpp 远未从整体上解决这个问题，但它希望能向前迈出一小步。

在致谢中，作者特别感谢了几位贡献者：Mozilla AI 及其 BiR 项目以及工程师 Davide，当该项目还只是一个模糊的想法时，他就决定支持他； ggml 及其贡献者，他们构成了整个项目的基础； Modal，为WER测试和CUDA验证提供计算学分； Blacksmith，负责处理部分 CI/CD； Hugging Face，既是当地人工智能社区的支柱，又为掌机组织提供了模型存储的私人空间。 至于AI是否辅助开发，作者很直接地回答：当然有，但是一个人不可能在几个月内仅使用ggml从头开始创建一个如此规模的引擎。 然而，这里呈现的文本都不是由人工智能编写的；一切都是他亲口亲手写的。

原文链接：workshop.cjpais.com/projects/transcribe-cpp
